title_translated: 基于恶化技巧将重写规则编译为有限状态转换器
summary_translated: 有限状态转换器（FST）在计算语言学和自然语言处理（NLP）中对于建模字符串重写至关重要，特别是在音位学和形态学重写规则方面。由于匹配重叠和上下文约束，将形式为 $A \to B / L \, \_ \, R$ 的通用重写规则（其中 $A$、$B$、$L$ 和 $R$ 是任意正则语言）进行编译非常复杂。传统方法（如Kaplan和Kay或Karttunen的方法）依赖于带有辅助标记的复杂转换器组合。本文提出一种基于“恶化技巧”的紧凑编译方案：生成所有合法的重写候选，然后过滤掉那些在相同输入下表现不如其他候选的项。该方法作为PyFoma内置重写编译器实现，支持多种上下文、任意转换、标记、定向重写、权重和并行重写。生成的公式简短且统一，在语义一致时可复现早期方法的规则转换器，同时更易于扩展。该实现已在大量重写语法集和覆盖主要重写模式的自动化回归测试套件上通过foma验证，结果转换器除状态编号外完全一致。

summary: Language models increasingly serve as the backbone of text-to-speech (TTS) systems, yet we understand little about the representations they build when text and generated speech tokens share a single residual stream. We train BatchTopK sparse autoencoders on the LM backbone of CosyVoice3 and introduce a modality-aware auto-interp pipeline that labels each feature from where it fires-text-prefix context, 1-second speech clips, or both. The recovered features are interpretable, spanning phonemes, laughter, accent prompts and speaker gender. Steering through the SAE latent space shows these features are causal rather than merely descriptive: targeted interventions raise laughter probability from 0.02 to 0.79, flip perceived speaker gender, and control speech rate while preserving spoken content. SAE features thus serve both as interpretability objects and as control directions for TTS synthesis.
title: Interpreting and Steering a Text-to-Speech Language Model with Sparse Autoencoders
title_translated: 通过稀疏自编码器解释与引导文本转语音语言模型
summary_translated: 语言模型越来越多地作为文本转语音（TTS）系统的核心，但我们对其在文本与生成语音标记共享单一残差流时所构建表示的理解仍十分有限。我们在CosyVoice3的语言模型主干上训练了BatchTopK稀疏自编码器，并引入一种模态感知的自动解释管道，用于标注每个特征的触发来源——文本前缀上下文、1秒语音片段或两者兼有。恢复出的特征具有可解释性，涵盖音素、笑声、口音提示和说话人性别。通过稀疏自编码器潜在空间的引导表明，这些特征是因果性的而非仅描述性：针对性干预可使笑声概率从0.02提升至0.79，反转感知的说话人性别，并控制语音速率同时保持内容不变。因此，SAE特征既可用作可解释性对象，也可作为TTS合成的控制方向。

summary: We introduce RKSC (Reasoning-Aware KV Cache Sharing), a training-free inference framework that eliminates two structural redundancies in multi-branch LLM reasoning pipelines. ASKS (Attention-Similarity KV Sharing) computes the prefix KV cache once and broadcasts it to all semantically similar branches via hidden-state cosine similarity, strictly generalising the token-exact prefix caching used by vLLM and SGLang. CGEE (Confidence-Gated Early Exit) applies two complementary exit mechanisms: (1) it skips the verification forward pass entirely when generation confidence is decisive across branches, and (2) it terminates the verification pass at an intermediate layer when per-layer entropy stabilises, using lightweight hooks on the transformer backbone. RSBCM (Reasoning-Selective Block Cache Manager) prevents unbounded cache growth via attention-weighted depth-priority eviction. Across five model families (7B-10B), four benchmarks, and 1,000 evaluated problems, RKSC achieves a mean speedup of 3.008x over the No-KV baseline (peak 3.990x), a 1.66x mean improvement over vLLM-equivalent prefix caching, with a CGEE-induced error rate of only 0.37% (6 errors out of 1,616 verify calls). No fine-tuning or architecture changes are required. Code is available at https://github.com/AnirudhSekar/RKSC.
title: RKSC: Reasoning-Aware KV Cache Sharing and Confident Early Exit for Multi-Step LLM Inference
comment: Accepted to the ICML 2026 Workshop on Statistical Frameworks for Uncertainty in Agentic Systems
title_translated: 面向多步LLM推理的推理感知KV缓存共享与置信度早退机制
summary_translated: 我们提出了RKSC（推理感知KV缓存共享），一种无需训练的推理框架，旨在消除多分支LLM推理流水线中的两种结构性冗余。ASKS（注意力相似性KV共享）仅计算一次前缀KV缓存，并通过隐藏状态余弦相似度广播至所有语义相似分支，严格推广了vLLM和SGLang使用的精确token前缀缓存。CGEE（置信度门控早退）采用两种互补的退出机制：（1）当各分支生成置信度明确时，完全跳过验证前向传播；（2）当逐层熵趋于稳定时，通过轻量级钩子在中间层终止验证过程。RSBCM（推理选择性块缓存管理器）通过注意力加权的深度优先淘汰策略防止缓存无限增长。在五个模型家族（7B-10B）、四个基准和1000个评估问题上，RKSC相比无KV基线平均提速3.008倍（峰值达3.990倍），比vLLM等价前缀缓存平均提升1.66倍，CGEE导致的错误率仅为0.37%（1616次验证调用中仅6次错误）。整个方法无需微调或架构修改。代码已开源：https://github.com/AnirudhSekar/RKSC。

summary: Post-training quantization (PTQ) is one of the most practical ways to reduce the serving cost of Large Language Models (LLMs), but activation quantization remains difficult because outlier-dominated channels lead to large quantization errors. This paper investigates whether part of this degradation is caused by over-migration in scaling-based equivalent transformations. We introduce a quantile-robust scaling policy for SmoothRot-style transforms by replacing max-based activation statistics with high quantiles, and we complement it with constrained gradient-based optimization of channel scales. On LLaMA-3.2-1B under W4A4 quantization, quantile-only policy search improves selected-layer error by 11.1% over the SmoothRot baseline, joint (alpha, q) search improves it by 12%, and training reaches 18.5%. Replaying the best selected-layer policy on all decoder-block down-projection layers reduces the corresponding full-layer mean error from 97.51 to 78.08 (19.9%). The results show that robust migration control and lightweight scale learning provide consistent gains over max-based fixed policies while preserving the equivalent-transform framework.
title: Trainable Smooth-Rotation Transforms with Learned Channel Scales for LLM Quantization
comment: 6 pages, 8 figures, 3 tables. Accepted to IEEE INES 2026 conference proceedings
title_translated: 用于LLM量化训练的可学习平滑旋转变换与学习通道尺度
summary_translated: 后训练量化（PTQ）是降低大语言模型（LLMs）服务成本最实用的方法之一，但激活量化仍具挑战性，因为主导通道的异常值会导致较大的量化误差。本文探讨了部分性能下降是否由基于缩放的等效变换中过度迁移引起。我们提出一种针对SmoothRot型变换的分位数鲁棒缩放策略，用高分位数替代最大值作为激活统计量，并辅以通道尺度的约束梯度优化。在LLaMA-3.2-1B模型上使用W4A4量化时，仅分位数策略搜索使选定层误差降低11.1%，联合（alpha, q）搜索降低12%，训练达到18.5%。将最佳选定层策略应用于所有解码器块的降维层，使对应全层平均误差从97.51降至78.08（降幅19.9%）。结果表明，鲁棒的迁移控制与轻量级尺度学习在保留等效变换框架的同时，持续优于基于最大值的固定策略。

summary: Across contemplative, philosophical, and psychological accounts, human consciousness is often described along a similar spectrum, ranging from reactive and self-focused patterns to more integrative and coherent ones. Understanding whether language models encode such a structured, human-interpretable consciousness spectrum in representation space is important for model guidance, evaluation and alignment. In this work, we study the geometric structure and dynamics of patterns along this spectrum in transformer embedding spaces. We show that embeddings exhibit a globally organized geometry aligned with this spectrum: sentences associated with similar states cluster into locally coherent regions, forming a structured manifold. In particular, higher-level and lower-level regions exhibit convexity-like stability, while intermediate regions form a transition corridor. Dynamically, both utility-guided and geometry-only greedy trajectories consistently traverse from lower- to higher-level regions, passing through intermediate tiers, indicating that navigability is an intrinsic property of the representation space, guided but not dictated by a global directional signal. These results suggest that embedding spaces encode structured and navigable geometry aligned with a hypothesized consciousness-spectrum taxonomy, broadly inspired by recurring structural descriptions of human consciousness across contemplative traditions, philosophy, and modern psychology, providing a representation-level perspective for analyzing and guiding model behavior.
title: A Navigable Manifold of Hypothesized Consciousness-Spectrum States in Language Model Representations
title_translated: 语言模型表征中可导航的假说意识谱状态流形
summary_translated: 在冥想、哲学和心理学论述中，人类意识常被描述为一个从反应性、自我聚焦到更整合、更连贯状态的连续谱。理解语言模型是否在表征空间中编码了这种结构化、人类可解释的意识谱，对模型引导、评估与对齐至关重要。本文研究了变压器嵌入空间中沿该谱的模式的几何结构与动态特性。我们发现嵌入表现出与该谱对齐的全局组织几何结构：与相似状态相关的句子聚集成局部连贯区域，形成一个结构化的流形。特别是，高层与低层区域表现出类似凸性的稳定性，而中间区域构成过渡走廊。动态上，无论是基于效用引导还是仅基于几何的贪婪轨迹，均一致地从低层向高层区域穿越，经过中间层级，表明导航性是表征空间的内在属性，受全局方向信号引导但非其决定。这些结果表明，嵌入空间编码了与假说意识谱分类法对齐的结构化且可导航的几何，广泛受到不同冥想传统、哲学及现代心理学中反复出现的人类意识结构描述启发，为分析和引导模型行为提供了表征层面的新视角。

summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents capable of executing multi-step action trajectories toward a given objective. While existing safety research has focused on detecting unethical behavior from complete trajectories, this paradigm is fundamentally retrospective: it identifies harm only after it has already occurred. In this work, we study a critical yet overlooked safety task, which we term Predictive Monitoring: given only a partial action trajectory, can a model infer whether it will culminate in an unethical action before the overt action is executed? To support this task, we present PreActBench, a benchmark of 1,000 paired ethical and unethical action trajectories spanning five domains. We evaluate a range of LLMs, safety guardrail models, and latent probing methods across varying fractions of the action trajectory using our Prefix Foresight F1 metric. Results show that while humans achieve promising performance, predictive monitoring remains challenging even for strong models, highlighting the need for future-oriented risk reasoning in LLM safety.
title: PreAct-Bench: Benchmarking Predictive Monitoring in LLMs
title_translated: PreAct-Bench：大语言模型预测性监控的基准测试
summary_translated: 大型语言模型（LLMs）越来越多地被用作能够执行多步动作轨迹以达成目标的自主智能体。尽管现有的安全研究主要关注从完整动作轨迹中检测不道德行为，但这种范式本质上是回顾性的：只有在危害已经发生后才能识别。本文研究了一个关键却被忽视的安全任务，称为预测性监控：仅基于部分动作轨迹，模型能否在明显动作执行前推断出该轨迹是否会最终导致不道德行为？为此，我们提出了PreActBench，一个包含1000对伦理与非伦理动作轨迹的基准，涵盖五个领域。我们使用Prefix Foresight F1指标，在不同动作轨迹比例下评估了多种LLM、安全护栏模型和潜在探测方法。结果表明，虽然人类表现良好，但即使是强模型也难以实现有效的预测性监控，凸显了在LLM安全中需要具备前瞻性风险推理能力。

summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) for large language models usually supervises reasoning with scalar outcome rewards, such as binary correctness. Such rewards provide an optimization direction but rarely explain how a model should revise its mistaken reasoning, which can encourage shortcut learning and brittle policies. We propose \textbf{SocraticPO} (Socratic Policy Optimization), a policy-optimization framework that augments RL rollouts with Socratic-style natural-language guidance. During rollout, the student first answers independently; if the answer is incorrect, a teacher diagnoses the attempt and provides concise corrective guidance, after which the student continues under the expanded context. Crucially, this guidance is paired with reward decay: correct answers obtained after teacher intervention only receive decayed rewards, preventing the policy from treating teacher help as a free path to reward. Since SocraticPO only modifies the rollout process while leaving the standard expected-reward objective intact, it can be plugged into existing policy-gradient backends such as Reinforce++. Moreover, because the teacher provides only text-level guidance, SocraticPO can leverage stronger black-box teacher models without requiring access to logits or distribution matching. On undergraduate-level scientific reasoning benchmarks from SciKnowEval, SocraticPO improves over strong RL and self-distillation baselines. Ablations show that both targeted guidance and reward decay are necessary, with reward decay mitigating reliance on assisted correction.
title: SocraticPO: Policy Optimization via Interactive Guidance
title_translated: SocraticPO：通过交互式指导进行策略优化
summary_translated: 大语言模型的强化学习通常以标量结果奖励（如二元正确性）来监督推理过程。这类奖励虽提供优化方向，但很少解释模型应如何修正错误推理，容易引发捷径学习和脆弱策略。我们提出	extbf{SocraticPO}（苏格拉底策略优化），一种增强强化学习轨迹的框架，引入苏格拉底风格的自然语言指导。在轨迹执行过程中，学生先独立作答；若答案错误，教师会诊断并提供简洁的纠正指导，随后学生在扩展上下文中继续作答。关键在于，这种指导与奖励衰减相结合：经教师干预后得出的正确答案仅获得衰减后的奖励，防止策略将教师帮助视为获取奖励的免费路径。由于SocraticPO仅修改轨迹生成过程而保持标准期望奖励目标不变，因此可无缝集成到现有策略梯度后端（如Reinforce++）。此外，因教师仅提供文本级指导，SocraticPO可利用更强的黑盒教师模型，无需访问logits或分布匹配。在SciKnowEval的本科级科学推理基准上，SocraticPO优于强大的强化学习和自蒸馏基线。消融实验表明，针对性指导和奖励衰减均必不可少，其中奖励衰减有效缓解对辅助修正的依赖。

summary: LLM wiki systems compile knowledge into pre-filled KV caches for efficient inference, but assume a static corpus -- an assumption that fails whenever the underlying information landscape evolves. We formalize Streaming Knowledge Compilation: given a document stream, a fixed token budget, and future queries unknown at ingestion time, maintain a compiled wiki that minimizes cumulative regret against an offline oracle with perfect foresight. The enabling insight is a materiality signal $φ_t(k,n)\in[0,1]$ that scores document importance for entity $k$ at time $t$, acting as a query-relevance surrogate for proactive pinning before queries arrive; we prove an $O(\sqrt{T\log K})$ regret bound where $\varepsilon=\mathbb{E}[|φ_t-\hatφ_t|]$ is the only domain-specific quantity. We instantiate in two domains: finance, where $φ_t$ is abnormal stock volatility predicted by frozen Llama 3.1 8B classification head (AUROC = 0.728 on 76K articles, strict temporal split; $1.49\times$ higher realized forward volatility for predicted-material articles); and Wikipedia, where $φ_t$ is the Abnormal Edit Ratio (AER), a cross-sectionally normalized edit velocity -- showing the same algorithm generalizes beyond the finance domain. End-to-end QA evaluation on 173 matched pairs (finance) and 119 (Wikipedia) reveals a pervasive LLM-as-judge confound on post-training knowledge, establishing that regret analysis -- not absolute QA scores -- is the reliable evaluation metric for compiled knowledge systems. Finance cumulative regret converges to -20.0 (-0.12/step); Wikipedia to +16.0 (+0.13/step), with the positive sign confirming that Wikipedia edit content is genuinely post-training -- richer context consistently improves scores (No Wiki 3.80 vs. Oracle 4.74) -- and eliminates this confound. The $O(\sqrt{T\log K})$ guarantee applies to any domain where knowledge gaps can be predicted from streaming signals.
title: Streaming Knowledge Compilation: Proactive Materiality-Scored Pinning for Time-Evolving LLM Wikis
title_translated: 流式知识编译：面向时变LLM维基的主动重要性评分固定机制
summary_translated: LLM维基系统将知识编译为预填充的KV缓存以实现高效推理，但其假设语料库静态不变——这一假设在底层信息环境演变时失效。我们形式化了流式知识编译：给定文档流、固定的token预算以及未知的未来查询，在摄入时维护一个编译维基，使其累积遗憾最小化，相对于拥有完美预见能力的离线Oracle。核心洞察是一个重要性信号$φ_t(k,n)∈[0,1]$，用于评分实体$k$在时间$t$的重要性，作为主动固定前的查询相关性代理；我们证明了$O(√{T\log K})$的遗憾界，其中$ε=ℕ[|φ_t-ς_t|]$是唯一依赖领域的量。我们在两个领域中实现：金融领域，$φ_t$由冻结的Llama 3.1 8B分类头预测异常股票波动率（在76K篇文章上AUROC=0.728，严格时间划分；预测重要文章的实际前向波动率高出1.49倍）；维基百科领域，$φ_t$为异常编辑比率（AER），即跨截面标准化的编辑速度——表明该算法可泛化至金融以外领域。在173对（金融）和119对（维基百科）的端到端问答评估中，揭示了后训练知识中的普遍性LLM作为裁判偏差，确立了遗憾分析（而非绝对QA分数）是编译知识系统的可靠评估指标。金融领域累积遗憾收敛至-20.0（-0.12/步）；维基百科收敛至+16.0（+0.13/步），正号确认维基百科编辑内容确为后训练内容——更丰富的上下文始终提升得分（无维基3.80 vs. Oracle 4.74），并消除该偏差。$O(√{T\log K})$保证适用于任何可通过流信号预测知识缺口的领域。

summary: High-quality source code documentation is vital yet often neglected, especially in critical domains like healthcare where reliability and maintainability are essential. We presented an AI powered framework that automates documentation generation from code and repositories using eight state of the art Large Language Models (LLMs), including GPT, Gemini, Qwen, and LLaMA variants. Built on the PocketFlow orchestration framework, the system applies modular pipelines and advanced prompt engineering to produce structured, context aware documentation. To ensure quality and guide model selection, we introduced a MultiLLMasJudges evaluation framework, where four independent LLMs assess outputs across nine criteria, such as Completeness, Clarity, and Faithfulness. Experiments conducted on an open-source medical physics library, demonstrated showed a 42% performance gap between top and bottom models. By combining diverse model outputs, optimized prompting, and rigorous evaluation, our approach enhances documentation quality and reduces manual effort, especially in safety critical healthcare software.
title: LLM-Based Code Documentation Generation and Multi-Judge Evaluation

title_translated: 基于大语言模型的代码文档生成与多裁判评估
summary_translated: 高质量源代码文档至关重要，但在医疗等关键领域常被忽视，因为可靠性与可维护性尤为关键。我们提出一个AI驱动的框架，利用八种最先进的大语言模型（包括GPT、Gemini、Qwen和LLaMA系列）自动从代码和仓库中生成文档。该系统基于PocketFlow编排框架，采用模块化流水线和高级提示工程，生成结构化且上下文感知的文档。为确保质量并指导模型选择，我们引入了MultiLLMasJudges评估框架，四个独立的LLM从完整性、清晰性、忠实性等九个维度评估输出。在开源医学物理库上的实验表明，顶尖模型与最差模型之间存在42%的性能差距。通过结合多样模型输出、优化提示设计和严格评估，我们的方法显著提升了文档质量并减少了人工工作量，尤其适用于安全关键型医疗软件。

summary: Post-training alignment algorithms are predominantly evaluated as black boxes, obscuring how they reshape language models' internal computations. We present a systematic mechanistic analysis of six preference-optimization methods: PPO, DPO, SimPO, ORPO, GRPO, and KTO across three open-weight model families. By integrating layer-wise linear probing, Sparse Autoencoders, and crosscoders, we localize preference representations and quantify alignment-induced geometric transformations in latent space. We find that preference signals consistently concentrate in early--mid or mid--late layers, but different objectives induce qualitatively distinct representational shifts. KTO and GRPO enhance linear separability through constructive feature sharing and sparse, high-salience recruitment. In contrast, DPO and ORPO degrade separability via non-constructive geometric rotation and feature attenuation, while PPO and SimPO largely preserve baseline geometry. These transformations exhibit architecture-dependent variability, demonstrating that behavioral alignment does not imply uniform internal restructuring. Our findings establish alignment as a heterogeneous intervention, motivate standardized feature-level auditing for safety and interpretability, and highlight the need for mechanism-aware optimization objectives.
title: Mechanistic Analysis of Alignment Algorithms in Language Models
title_translated: 语言模型对齐算法的机制分析
summary_translated: 后训练对齐算法通常作为黑箱进行评估，掩盖了它们如何重塑语言模型内部计算过程。我们对六种偏好优化方法（PPO、DPO、SimPO、ORPO、GRPO、KTO）在三个开源权重模型族中的表现进行了系统性的机制分析。通过整合层间线性探测、稀疏自编码器和交叉编码器，我们定位了偏好表示，并量化了隐空间中对齐引起的几何变换。研究发现，偏好信号一致集中在早期-中期或中期-晚期层，但不同目标引发质性不同的表征转变。KTO和GRPO通过建设性特征共享和稀疏高显著性特征招募增强了线性可分性；相比之下，DPO和ORPO通过非建设性几何旋转和特征衰减降低了可分性，而PPO和SimPO则基本保持基线几何结构。这些变换表现出架构依赖性差异，表明行为对齐并不意味着统一的内部重构。我们的发现确立了对齐是一种异质性干预，推动了针对特征层面的安全与可解释性审计标准化，并强调了机制感知优化目标的必要性。

summary: Visual art remains largely inaccessible to blind and low-vision (BLV) audiences due to brief or absent alt-text, which rarely conveys the sensory, spatial, or emotional qualities of an artwork. This study presents an automated workflow that generates multi-sensory art descriptions and synchronized audio narration using large language models and text-to-speech services. The system, orchestrated through Zapier, converts uploaded images into rich narrative captions without human intervention, enabling rapid, scalable production of accessible media. Quantitative evaluation across 50 artworks shows that AI-generated descriptions contain significantly higher lexical diversity, adjective density, and narrative detail than baseline captions, while maintaining comparable readability levels. Statistical tests (t-tests, ANOVA) confirm meaningful differences in richness and length, and the full pipeline produces text-plus-audio outputs in under 20 seconds per image at a cost below $0.05. Findings demonstrate that automated captioning can bridge gaps in museum and digital-collection accessibility, with implications for broader public engagement. Future work can incorporate user studies with BLV participants to assess comprehension, preference, and optimal levels of interpretive language.
title: CANVAS: Captioning Art with Narrative Visual-Audio AI Systems
title_translated: CANVAS：使用叙事视觉-音频AI系统为艺术作品生成描述
summary_translated: 由于缺乏或仅有简短的替代文本，视觉艺术对盲人和低视力（BLV）观众而言仍难以访问，而这些替代文本很少能传达作品的感官、空间或情感特征。本研究提出了一种自动化工作流程，利用大语言模型和文本转语音服务生成多感官艺术描述并同步音频叙述。该系统通过Zapier协调，无需人工干预即可将上传的图像转换为丰富的叙事性标题，实现可快速扩展的无障碍媒体生产。在50件艺术品上的定量评估显示，AI生成的描述在词汇多样性、形容词密度和叙事细节方面显著优于基线标题，同时保持相当的可读性水平。统计检验（t检验、方差分析）证实了丰富程度和长度上的显著差异，整个流水线每幅图像的输出时间低于20秒，成本低于0.05美元。研究结果表明，自动化描述能够弥合博物馆和数字收藏在可访问性方面的差距，对更广泛的公众参与具有重要意义。未来工作可结合BLV用户的研究，评估理解度、偏好以及解释性语言的最佳水平。

summary: Large language models (LLMs) produce stable self-reports on personality inventories, but these self-reports do not predict observed behavior. Whether this gap reflects a mismatch between LLMs and human trait constructs, or a deeper property of LLM self-report itself, has been unresolved. We constructed the first psychometric instrument whose constructs are derived bottom-up from LLM behavioral affordances via exploratory factor analysis (EFA). We administered 300 items (240 direct Likert + 60 scenario-based) spanning 12 candidate behavioral dimensions to 25 LLMs across 17 model families, each item administered 30 times. EFA yielded a 5-factor structure -- Responsiveness, Deference, Boldness, Guardedness, and Verbosity -- with excellent split-half replicability (all Tucker $φ\geq .957$) and internal consistency (all $α\geq .930$). To test predictive validity, we collected 2,500 open-ended behavioral samples rated by 151 human raters and a three-judge LLM ensemble. Human and judge ratings agreed ($\bar{r} = .51$), but neither tracked self-report: self-report--human $\bar{r} = -.01$, self-report--judge $\bar{r} = .13$, with no factor-level self-report--human CI excluding zero. On Responsiveness, self-report correlated with LLM judges ($r = .53$) but not humans ($r = .04$), even though humans and judges agreed ($r = .59$) -- indicating self-report items and LLM judges share variance that human observers do not, a confound invisible to within-ensemble reliability checks. We release the instrument as a diagnostic probe for alignment-shaped self-description and a concrete risk factor for LLM-as-judge pipelines.
title: An LLM-Native Psychometric Instrument Does Not Predict LLM Behavior: Evidence Across 25 Models
title_translated: 基于LLM原生的心理测量工具无法预测LLM行为：跨25个模型的证据
summary_translated: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在人格量表上会产生稳定的自我报告，但这些自我报告并不能预测其实际行为。这一差距究竟是源于LLM与人类特质构念之间的不匹配，还是LLM自我报告本身更深层的属性，尚无定论。我们构建了首个心理测量工具，其构念通过探索性因子分析（EFA）自下而上地从LLM的行为能力中提取。我们向25个LLM（涵盖17个模型家族）施测了300项题目（240项直接李克特量表+60项情境题），每项题目重复30次。EFA得出一个五因素结构——响应性、顺从性、大胆性、谨慎性和冗长性，具有极佳的折半信度（所有Tucker φ ≥ .957）和内部一致性（所有α ≥ .930）。为检验预测效度，我们收集了2500个开放式行为样本，由151名人类评分员和三名评委LLM集合进行评分。人类评分员与评委间评分一致（平均相关系数 r = .51），但两者均未与自我报告相关：自我报告-人类平均相关系数 r = -.01，自我报告-评委平均相关系数 r = .13，且无任何因子层面的自我报告-人类置信区间排除零值。在响应性维度上，自我报告与LLM评委相关（r = .53），但与人类无关（r = .04），尽管人类与评委之间高度一致（r = .59）——这表明自我报告题目与LLM评委共享方差，而人类观察者并不具备这种共变，这是一个在内部一致性检查中无法察觉的混淆因素。我们公开该工具作为对对齐型自我描述的诊断探针，以及LLM作为评判者流程中的具体风险因素。

summary: Large audio language models (LALMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world applications, yet their safety alignment is still primarily evaluated on monolingual, text-based harmful prompts. This leaves their generalizability under multilingual and spoken settings, particularly code-switched speech, largely underexplored. To address this gap, we introduce SpeechJBB, an audio jailbreak dataset for benchmarking across multiple state-of-the-art LALMs. The extent of safety weaknesses is further probed by introducing an augmented setting where phonologically plausible pseudo-words are inserted around safety-critical terms to simulate localized obfuscation. Across models, code-switched harmful audio yields substantially high jailbreak success rates (JSR), with non-English monolingual and non-English code-switched pairs exhibiting the highest attack success. Pseudo-word insertion further reduces refusal rates, which demonstrates that natural-sounding obfuscation can effectively bypass safety policies.
title: SpeechJBB: Probing Safety Alignment and Comprehension in Large Audio Language Models under Code-Switched Speech
title_translated: SpeechJBB：在代码混用语音下探测大型音频语言模型的安全对齐与理解能力
summary_translated: 大型音频语言模型（LALMs）在现实应用中日益普及，但其安全对齐性仍主要基于单语、基于文本的有害提示进行评估。这使得它们在多语言和口语场景下的泛化能力，特别是代码混用语音情况下的表现，仍基本未被探索。为填补这一空白，我们引入SpeechJBB，一个用于基准测试多个前沿LALMs的音频越狱数据集。通过引入一种增强设置，在关键安全术语周围插入音位上合理的伪词，以模拟局部混淆，进一步探测安全弱点。实验结果显示，代码混用的有害音频在各模型中均导致显著较高的越狱成功率（JSR），其中非英语单语和非英语代码混用组合的攻击成功率最高。伪词插入进一步降低了拒绝率，表明自然发音的混淆手段可有效绕过安全策略。
